framework.name: CIS Controls
framework.description: CIS Critical Security Controls v8 — prioritized set of actions for cyber defense

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.title: Establish and Maintain Detailed Enterprise Asset Inventory
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an accurate, detailed, and up-to-date inventory of all enterprise assets with the potential to store or process data, to include: end-user devices (including portable and mobile), network devices, non-computing/IoT devices, and servers. Ensure the inventory records the network address (if static), hardware address, machine name, enterprise asset owner, department for each asset, and whether the asset has been approved to connect to the network. For mobile end-user devices, MDM type tools can support this process, where appropriate. This inventory includes assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments. Additionally, it includes assets that are regularly connected to the enterprise's network infrastructure, even if they are not under control of the enterprise. Review and update the inventory of all enterprise assets bi-annually, or more frequently.
evidenceRequirements: Asset inventory report from CMDB or asset management tool

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.title: Address Unauthorized Assets
controls.requirementText: Ensure that a process exists to address unauthorized assets on a weekly basis. The enterprise may choose to remove the asset from the network, deny the asset from connecting remotely to the network, or quarantine the asset.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.title: Utilize an Active Discovery Tool
controls.requirementText: Utilize an active discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Configure the active discovery tool to execute daily, or more frequently.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.title: Use Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol (DHCP) Logging to Update Enterprise Asset Inventory
controls.requirementText: Use DHCP logging on all DHCP servers or Internet Protocol (IP) address management tools to update the enterprise's asset inventory. Review and use logs to update the enterprise's asset inventory weekly, or more frequently.
evidenceRequirements: Asset inventory update records from DHCP data

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
controls.title: Use a Passive Asset Discovery Tool
controls.requirementText: Use a passive discovery tool to identify assets connected to the enterprise's network. Review and use scans to update the enterprise's asset inventory at least weekly, or more frequently.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a detailed inventory of all licensed software installed on enterprise assets. The software inventory must document the title, publisher, initial install/use date, and business purpose for each entry; where appropriate, include the Uniform Resource Locator (URL), app store(s), version(s), deployment mechanism, and decommission date. Review and update the software inventory bi-annually, or more frequently.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.title: Ensure Authorized Software is Currently Supported
controls.requirementText: Ensure that only currently supported software is designated as authorized in the software inventory for enterprise assets. If software is unsupported, yet necessary for the fulfillment of the enterprise's mission, document an exception detailing mitigating controls and residual risk acceptance. For any unsupported software without an exception documentation, designate as unauthorized. Review the software list to verify software support at least monthly, or more frequently.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.title: Address Unauthorized Software
controls.requirementText: Ensure that unauthorized software is either removed from use on enterprise assets or receives a documented exception. Review monthly, or more frequently.

controls.domain: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
controls.title: Allowlist Authorized Software
controls.requirementText: Use technical controls, such as application allowlisting, to ensure that only authorized software can execute or be accessed. Reassess bi-annually, or more frequently.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Management Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a data management process. In the process, address data sensitivity, data owner, handling of data, data retention limits, and disposal requirements, based on sensitivity and retention standards for the enterprise. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Configure Data Access Control Lists
controls.requirementText: Configure data access control lists based on a user's need to know. Apply data access control lists, also known as access permissions, to local and remote file systems, databases, and applications.

controls.title: Enforce Data Retention
controls.requirementText: Retain data according to the enterprise's data management process. Data retention must include both minimum and maximum timelines.

controls.title: Encrypt Data on End-User Devices
controls.requirementText: Encrypt data on end-user devices containing sensitive data. Example implementations can include: Windows BitLocker, Apple FileVault, Linux dm-crypt.

controls.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data in Transit
controls.requirementText: Encrypt sensitive data in transit. Example implementations can include: Transport Layer Security (TLS) and Open Secure Shell (OpenSSH).

controls.title: Encrypt Sensitive Data at Rest
controls.requirementText: Encrypt sensitive data at rest on servers, applications, and databases containing sensitive data. Storage-layer encryption, also known as server-side encryption, meets the minimum requirement of this Safeguard. Additional encryption methods may include application-layer encryption, also known as client-side encryption, where access to the data storage device does not grant access to the plain-text data.

controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a secure configuration process for enterprise assets (end-user devices, including portable and mobile; non-computing/IoT devices; and servers) and software (operating systems and applications). Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.title: Configure Automatic Session Locking on Enterprise Assets
controls.requirementText: Configure automatic session locking on enterprise assets after a defined period of inactivity. For general purpose operating systems, the period must not exceed 15 minutes. For mobile end-user devices, the period must not exceed 2 minutes.

controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.title: Implement and Manage a Firewall on Servers
controls.requirementText: Implement and manage a firewall on servers, where supported. Example implementations include a virtual firewall, operating system firewall, or a third-party firewall agent.

controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.title: Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.requirementText: Manage default accounts on enterprise assets and software, such as root, administrator, and other pre-configured vendor accounts. Example implementations can include: disabling default accounts or making them unusable.

controls.domain: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.title: Uninstall or Disable Unnecessary Services on Enterprise Assets and Software
controls.requirementText: Uninstall or disable unnecessary services on enterprise assets and software, such as an unused file sharing service, web application module, or service function.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an inventory of all accounts managed in the enterprise. The inventory must include both user and administrator accounts. The inventory, at a minimum, should contain the person's name, username, start/stop dates, department, and active/disabled status. Validate that all active accounts are authorized, on a recurring schedule at a minimum quarterly, or more frequently.

controls.title: Use Unique Passwords
controls.requirementText: Use unique passwords for all enterprise assets. Best practice implementation includes, at a minimum, an 8-character password for accounts using MFA and a 14-character password for accounts not using MFA.

controls.title: Disable Dormant Accounts
controls.requirementText: Delete or disable any dormant accounts after a period of 45 days of inactivity, where supported.

controls.title: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
controls.requirementText: Restrict administrator privileges to dedicated administrator accounts on enterprise assets. Conduct general computing activities, such as internet browsing, email, and productivity suite use, from the user's primary, non-privileged account.

controls.title: Establish an Access Granting Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for granting access to enterprise assets upon new hire, rights grant, or role change of a user.

controls.title: Establish an Access Revoking Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and follow a process, preferably automated, for revoking access to enterprise assets, through disabling accounts, instead of deleting accounts, upon termination, rights revocation, or role change of a user. Disable accounts, instead of deleting accounts, to preserve audit trails.

controls.title: Require MFA for Externally-Exposed Applications
controls.requirementText: Require all externally-exposed enterprise applications to enforce MFA, where supported. Enforcing MFA through a directory service or SSO provider is a satisfactory implementation of this Safeguard.
evidenceRequirements: External application inventory with MFA status

controls.title: Require MFA for Remote Network Access
controls.requirementText: Require MFA for remote network access.

controls.title: Require MFA for Administrative Access
controls.requirementText: Require MFA for all administrative access accounts, where supported, on all enterprise assets, whether managed on-site or through a third-party provider.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Vulnerability Management Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a documented vulnerability management process for enterprise assets. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Remediation Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a risk-based remediation strategy documented in a remediation process, with monthly, or more frequent, parsimony of relevant parts. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
controls.requirementText: Perform operating system updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly, or more frequent, basis.

controls.title: Perform Automated Application Patch Management
controls.requirementText: Perform application updates on enterprise assets through automated patch management on a monthly, or more frequent, basis.

controls.title: Perform Automated Vulnerability Scans of Internal Enterprise Assets
controls.requirementText: Perform automated vulnerability scans of internal enterprise assets on a quarterly, or more frequent, basis. Conduct both authenticated and unauthenticated scans, using a SCAP-compliant vulnerability scanning tool.

controls.title: Remediate Detected Vulnerabilities
controls.requirementText: Remediate detected vulnerabilities in software through processes and tooling on a monthly, or more frequent, basis, based on the remediation process.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Audit Log Management Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an audit log management process that defines the enterprise's logging requirements. At a minimum, address the collection, review, and retention of audit logs for enterprise assets. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Collect Audit Logs
controls.requirementText: Collect audit logs. Ensure that logging, per the enterprise's audit log management process, has been enabled across enterprise assets.

controls.title: Ensure Adequate Audit Log Storage
controls.requirementText: Ensure that logging destinations maintain adequate storage to comply with the enterprise's audit log management process.

controls.title: Collect Detailed Audit Logs
controls.requirementText: Configure detailed audit logging for enterprise assets containing sensitive data. Include event source, date, username, timestamp, source addresses, destination addresses, and other useful elements that could assist in a forensic investigation.

controls.title: Centralize Audit Logs
controls.requirementText: Centralize, to the extent possible, audit log collection and retention across enterprise assets.
evidenceRequirements: SIEM or log aggregator deployment records

controls.title: Conduct Audit Log Reviews
controls.requirementText: Conduct reviews of audit logs to detect anomalies or abnormal events that could indicate a potential threat. Conduct reviews on a weekly, or more frequent, basis.

controls.title: Ensure Use of Only Fully Supported Browsers and Email Clients
controls.requirementText: Ensure only fully supported browsers and email clients are allowed to execute in the enterprise, only using the latest version of browsers and email clients provided through the vendor.

controls.title: Use DNS Filtering Services
controls.requirementText: Use DNS filtering services on all enterprise assets to block access to known malicious domains.

controls.title: Deploy and Maintain Anti-Malware Software
controls.requirementText: Deploy and maintain anti-malware software on all enterprise assets.

controls.title: Configure Automatic Anti-Malware Signature Updates
controls.requirementText: Configure automatic updates for anti-malware signature files on all enterprise assets.

controls.title: Disable Autorun and Autoplay for Removable Media
controls.requirementText: Disable autorun and autoplay auto-execute functionality for removable media.
evidenceRequirements: GPO or MDM configuration for autorun/autoplay

controls.title: Use Behavior-Based Anti-Malware Software
controls.requirementText: Use behavior-based anti-malware software.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a data recovery process. In the process, address the scope of data recovery activities, recovery prioritization, and the security of backup data. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Perform Automated Backups
controls.requirementText: Perform automated backups of in-scope enterprise assets. Run backups weekly, or more frequently, based on the sensitivity and criticality of the enterprise asset.

controls.title: Protect Recovery Data
controls.requirementText: Protect recovery data with equivalent controls to the original data. Reference encryption or data separation, based on requirements.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an isolated instance of recovery data using air-gapped, cloud, or offline backup of recovery data. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Test Data Recovery
controls.requirementText: Test backup recovery quarterly, or more frequently, for a sampling of in-scope enterprise assets.

controls.title: Ensure Network Infrastructure is Up-to-Date
controls.requirementText: Ensure network infrastructure is kept up-to-date. Example implementations include running the latest stable release of software and/or using currently supported network-as-a-service (NaaS) offerings. Review software versions monthly, or more frequently, to verify software support.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Network Architecture
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a secure network architecture. A secure network architecture must address segmentation, least privilege, and availability, at a minimum.

controls.title: Centralize Security Event Alerting
controls.requirementText: Centralize security event alerting across enterprise assets for log correlation and analysis. Best practice implementation requires the use of a SIEM, which includes vendor-defined event correlation alerts. A log analytics platform configured with security-relevant correlation alerts also satisfies this Safeguard.

controls.title: Deploy a Network Intrusion Detection Solution
controls.requirementText: Deploy a network intrusion detection solution on enterprise assets, where appropriate. Example implementations include the use of a network intrusion detection system (NIDS) or equivalent cloud service provider (CSP) service.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Security Awareness Program
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a security awareness program. The purpose of a security awareness program is to educate the enterprise's workforce on how to interact with enterprise assets and data in a secure manner. Conduct training at hire and at a minimum annually. Review and update content annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members to Recognize Social Engineering Attacks
controls.requirementText: Train workforce members to recognize social engineering attacks, such as phishing, pre-texting, and tailgating.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Authentication Best Practices
controls.requirementText: Train workforce members on authentication best practices. Example topics include MFA, password composition, and credential management.

controls.title: Train Workforce Members on Recognizing and Reporting Security Incidents
controls.requirementText: Train workforce members to be able to recognize a potential incident and be able to report such an incident.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Service Providers
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an inventory of service providers. The inventory is to list all known service providers, include classification(s), and designate an enterprise contact for each service provider. Review and update the inventory annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Ensure Service Provider Contracts Include Security Requirements
controls.requirementText: Ensure service provider contracts include security requirements. Example requirements may include, but are not limited to: minimum security standards, data processing and handling, incident notification and handling, and right to audit.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Secure Application Development Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a secure application development process. In the process, address such items as: secure application design standards, secure coding practices, developer training, vulnerability management, security of third-party code, and application security testing procedures. Review and update documentation annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Train Developers in Application Security Concepts and Secure Coding
controls.requirementText: Ensure all software development personnel receive training in writing secure code for their specific development environment and responsibilities. Training can include general security principles and application security standard practices. Conduct training at least annually and design in a way to promote security within the development team, and build a culture of security among the developers.

controls.title: Designate Personnel to Manage Incident Handling
controls.requirementText: Designate one key person, and at least one backup, who will manage the enterprise's incident handling process. Management personnel are responsible for the coordination and documentation of incident response and recovery efforts and can consist of employees internal to the enterprise, third-party vendors, or a hybrid approach. If using a third-party vendor, designate at least one person internal to the enterprise to oversee any third-party work. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain contact information for reporting security incidents. Contacts may include internal staff, third-party vendors, law enforcement, cyber insurance providers, relevant government agencies, Information Sharing and Analysis Center (ISAC) partners, or other stakeholders. Verify contacts annually to ensure that information is up-to-date.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Enterprise Process for Reporting Incidents
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an enterprise process for the workforce to report security incidents. The process includes reporting timeframe, personnel to report to, mechanism for reporting, and the minimum information to be reported. Ensure the process is publicly available to all of the workforce. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain an incident response process that addresses roles and responsibilities, compliance requirements, and a communication plan. Review annually, or when significant enterprise changes occur that could impact this Safeguard.

controls.title: Conduct Routine Incident Response Exercises
controls.requirementText: Conduct routine incident response exercises and scenarios for key personnel involved in the incident response process to prepare for responding to real-world incidents. Exercises need to test communication channels, decision making, and incident responder's technical capabilities using tools and data available to them. See supplementary guidance for recommended exercise scenarios.

controls.title: Conduct Post-Incident Reviews
controls.requirementText: Conduct post-incident reviews. Post-incident reviews help prevent incident recurrence through identifying lessons learned and follow-up action.

controls.title: Establish and Maintain a Penetration Testing Program
controls.requirementText: Establish and maintain a penetration testing program appropriate to the size, complexity, and maturity of the enterprise. Penetration testing program characteristics include scope, such as network, web application, Application Programming Interface (API), hosted services, and physical premise controls; frequency; limitations, such as acceptable hours, and excluded attack types; point of contact information; remediation, such as how findings will be routed internally; and retrospective requirements.

controls.title: Perform Periodic External Penetration Tests
controls.requirementText: Perform periodic external penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. External penetration testing must include enterprise and environmental reconnaissance to detect exploitable information. Penetration testing requires specialized skills and experience and must be conducted through a qualified party. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.

controls.title: Remediate Penetration Test Findings
controls.requirementText: Remediate penetration test findings based on the enterprise's policy for remediation scope and prioritization.

controls.title: Perform Periodic Internal Penetration Tests
controls.requirementText: Perform periodic internal penetration tests based on program requirements, no less than annually. The testing may be clear box or opaque box.
